macro.intro: The macroeconomic landscape is marked by rising inflation with CPI YoY at 330.29% and geopolitical tensions affecting energy markets. The Fed Funds Rate remains steady at 3.64%, while the 10Y TIPS Real Yield has slightly decreased to 1.92%. No major economic events are scheduled today, allowing markets to digest recent data and geopolitical developments.

snapshot.name: WTI Crude Oil
snapshot.note: Energy shock due to Iran conflict

snapshot.name: VIX
snapshot.note: Risk-off sentiment due to geopolitical risks

setups.name: Gold Long Setup
setups.rationale: Gold is gaining as a safe-haven asset amid geopolitical tensions, with prices at $1980.50. Rising CPI at 330.29% supports inflation hedging. No economic events today allow focus on geopolitical developments.

metrics.label: CPI Inflation
metrics.text: Rising inflation supports gold as a hedge

metrics.label: Economic event
metrics.text: No major events, focus on geopolitical

concerns.label: Primary risk
concerns.text: De-escalation in Iran could lower gold demand, pushing prices below $1950

concerns.label: Secondary risk
concerns.text: Unexpected Fed rate hike could strengthen USD

setups.hedge: Consider shorting EUR/USD at 1.1000 if gold declines.

setups.name: S&P 500 Short Setup
setups.rationale: The S&P 500 is nearing overbought levels at 4550, with recent gains driven by tech rallies. The 10Y-2Y yield spread narrowing to 0.5% signals caution.

concerns.label: Primary risk
concerns.text: Continued tech rally could push index above 4600

setups.hedge: Consider long VIX at 18.00 for volatility hedge.

setups.name: EUR/USD Long Setup
setups.rationale: EUR/USD is gaining as the dollar sheds Iran war premium, currently at 1.0950. The Fed's steady rate at 3.64% supports a weaker dollar outlook.

metrics.label: Economic event
metrics.text: No events, focus on USD movement

concerns.label: Primary risk
concerns.text: Unexpected USD strength could push EUR/USD below 1.0900

setups.hedge: Consider shorting GBP/USD at 1.3100 if EUR/USD declines.

contrarian.body: Despite current geopolitical tensions, WTI Crude Oil at $85.30 may face a correction if diplomatic efforts succeed. The EU warns of prolonged energy shock, but a ceasefire could rapidly change sentiment.
contrarian.warning: A sudden escalation in the Iran conflict could spike oil prices above $88.00. Monitor geopolitical developments closely.
